skill.name: audio-transcriber
skill.description: Transform audio recordings into professional Markdown documentation with intelligent summaries using LLM integration
skill.summary: Transform audio recordings into professional Markdown documentation with intelligent summaries using LLM integration
skill.author: Eric Andrade

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 266 potential issues, but evaluation reveals these are primarily false positives. The flagged patterns in documentation files (README.md, SKILL.md, CHANGELOG.md) represent code examples, not executable code. The actual Python script (transcribe.py) uses hardcoded CLI tool invocations for legitimate transcription purposes. No command injection vectors or malicious intent detected. This is a standard audio transcription utility.

medium_findings.title: External Command Execution in Documentation Examples
medium_findings.description: Static scanner flagged shell command examples in documentation files (README.md, SKILL.md, CHANGELOG.md). These are legitimate code examples for users to follow, not actual execution vectors.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are documentation files containing code example strings, not executable code. The backticks and command syntax are part of markdown formatting for displaying commands to users.

low_findings.title: Subprocess Calls with Hardcoded Arguments
low_findings.description: The transcribe.py script uses subprocess.run to invoke CLI tools (claude, gh copilot). All arguments are hardcoded strings, not user-controlled input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: subprocess.run(['claude', '-'], input=full_prompt) uses hardcoded command strings. No command injection risk as arguments are not derived from user input.

low_findings.title: File System Operations for Output
low_findings.description: Script writes transcription outputs to files. File paths are derived from input filename with timestamp appended.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard file writing operations using pathlib with timestamp-based filenames. No path traversal vulnerability as base filename comes from input argument.

content.value_statement: Converts audio recordings into searchable text and generates professional meeting notes with AI-powered summaries using Claude or GitHub Copilot integration.
actual_capabilities: Transcribe audio files (MP3, WAV, M4A) to text using OpenAI Whisper or Faster-Whisper engine
Generate structured Markdown meeting notes with timestamps and speaker identification
Process transcripts through LLM (Claude or GitHub Copilot) for intelligent summarization
Support multiple Whisper models (tiny, base, small, medium, large) for quality/speed tradeoffs
Auto-detect available AI CLI tools and prompt engineering workflows
limitations: Requires local Whisper model installation (first download may take time)
Audio quality affects transcription accuracy - clear audio produces better results
Processing long audio files can be slow without GPU acceleration
Requires Claude CLI or GitHub Copilot CLI installed for AI summarization features

use_cases.title: Meeting Transcription
use_cases.description: Automatically transcribe meeting recordings and generate professional meeting minutes with action items and decisions documented.
use_cases.target_user: Business professionals and team leads who need to document meetings

use_cases.title: Podcast and Interview Notes
use_cases.description: Convert podcast episodes or interview recordings into searchable text with AI-generated summaries for content creators.

use_cases.title: Lecture and Course Transcription
use_cases.description: Transform educational audio content into structured notes with key points extracted for study purposes.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Audio Transcription
prompt_templates.prompt: Transcribe this audio file to text. Output the complete transcription in Markdown format.

prompt_templates.title: Meeting Minutes Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this meeting transcription and create structured meeting minutes including: attendees, agenda topics discussed, key decisions made, and action items with owners.

prompt_templates.title: Executive Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Read this transcript and create a concise executive summary highlighting the main points, key takeaways, and any important announcements. Use bullet points for easy reading.

prompt_templates.title: Q&A Documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract all questions and answers from this interview or Q&A session. Format as a structured document with questions clearly separated from answers.

output_examples.output:
# Transcrição de Áudio

**Arquivo:** meeting-recording.mp3
**Idioma:** en
**Data:** 2024-01-15

---

## Transcrição Completa

**[00:00 → 00:15]**  Welcome everyone to our quarterly planning meeting.

**[00:15 → 00:45]**  Today we will discuss the roadmap for Q2 and assign tasks...

---

## Ata Gerada

**Resumo:** This quarterly planning meeting covered Q2 roadmap items including product launch timeline, resource allocation, and key milestones.

**Decisões:**
- Approved Q2 launch date of April 15th
- Team Alpha assigned to feature development
- Weekly sync meetings scheduled for Tuesdays

# Transcrição de Áudio

**Arquivo:** lecture-audio.wav
**Idioma:** pt
**Data:** 2024-01-20

---

## Pontos-Chave

1. **Conceitos Fundamentais** - Explanation of core concepts
2. **Aplicações Práticas** - Real-world applications
3. **Próximos Passos** - Recommended follow-up activities

best_practices: Use high-quality audio recordings for better transcription accuracy - minimize background noise
Start with the base Whisper model for fastest processing, upgrade to small or medium for improved accuracy
Provide custom prompts to guide the LLM output toward your desired format (meeting notes, summaries, Q&A)
anti_patterns: Do not expect perfect transcription of poor-quality audio with significant background noise
Avoid processing very long files without chunking - may cause memory issues
Do not skip the model download step if you want offline capability

faq.question: What audio formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports common audio formats including MP3, WAV, M4A, FLAC, and OGG. Any format readable by ffmpeg can be processed.

faq.question: Do I need to install Whisper manually?
faq.answer: No, the install-requirements.sh script will automatically install faster-whisper or openai-whisper. The base model will be downloaded on first use.

faq.question: Which is faster - faster-whisper or whisper?
faq.answer: Faster-Whisper is significantly faster and more memory-efficient as it uses CTranslate2. The script prioritizes faster-whisper and falls back to whisper if needed.

faq.question: Can I use this offline?
faq.answer: Yes, after downloading the Whisper model, transcription works completely offline. AI summarization requires Claude CLI or GitHub Copilot CLI to be installed.

faq.question: How accurate is the transcription?
faq.answer: Accuracy depends on audio quality and the model used. The base model provides good accuracy for clear audio. For better results, use small or medium models.

faq.question: What if neither Claude nor Copilot is installed?
faq.answer: The script will detect missing CLIs and save only the transcription text without AI-generated summaries. You can add summaries later with Claude or Copilot.
